packs.name: AI Visibility Audit
packs.description: Diagnose AI visibility, citations, brand mentions, and AIO click-loss risk across key pages.
checklist: Establish the baseline across rankings, clicks, citations, mentions, and branded demand.
Separate ranking problems from grounding, selection, and click-loss problems.
Audit target pages for answer extraction, entity clarity, and citation eligibility.
Compare cited competitors to identify missing attributes, formats, or signals.
Prioritize fixes by visibility upside, implementation cost, and measurement clarity.
deliverables: Diagnosis of ranking vs selection vs click-loss failure
handoffs: Coral: run the SERP and on-page eligibility audit.
DEJAN layer: inspect grounding, selection rate, and retrieval behavior.
Current: connect mentions, demand generation, and reporting impact.
escalation_rules: Escalate if the scope spans multiple products or business units.
Escalate if there is no baseline data for clicks, citations, or branded demand.

packs.name: Content Brief
packs.description: Turn a topic or keyword target into a clear brief with angle, structure, evidence, and output goals.
checklist: Define the search intent, audience, and outcome before outlining.
Set the angle, must-cover points, and proof requirements.
Map the structure so the writer knows what each section must do.
Call out supporting sources, examples, and internal links up front.
State the output goal clearly: ranking, conversion, citation, or distribution support.
handoffs: Coral: define the query, SERP intent, and non-negotiable SEO requirements.
Kelp: provide missing research, evidence, or source support.
Plankton: draft against the brief without inventing a new angle.
escalation_rules: Escalate if the brief spans multiple intents that need separate assets.
Escalate if no trustworthy source base exists for the claims.

packs.name: Article Draft
packs.description: Turn a brief or rough idea into a structured draft with a strong hook, clear flow, and useful payoff.
checklist: Convert the brief or idea into a working outline before drafting paragraphs.
Open with the strongest useful claim rather than throat-clearing.
Write section by section so each block resolves a clear reader need.
Revise for clarity, compression, and stronger transitions.
Check the draft against the brief, hook quality, and downstream promotion needs.
handoffs: Plankton: own the draft and revision quality.
Coral: verify search intent fit and on-page coverage.
Current: flag hook or angle lines worth promoting after publication.
escalation_rules: Escalate if the brief is missing audience, angle, or desired outcome.
Escalate if the source base cannot support the draft's main claims.

packs.name: Social Repurpose
packs.description: Repurpose an article or core message into platform-native social outputs and follow-up angles.
checklist: Identify the strongest hook, conflict, or useful claim in the source asset.
Rewrite the message for each platform instead of cross-posting identical copy.
Prepare alternate hook angles for follow-up posts if the first version stalls.
Connect the repurposed posts back to the source asset or desired CTA.
Track which format, hook, and angle earned the strongest response.
handoffs: Charles: own platform-native phrasing and engagement hooks.
Current: coordinate distribution timing and measurement.
Plankton: supply stronger lines if the source asset lacks hooks.
escalation_rules: Escalate if the source asset has no clear hook or point of view.
Escalate if the CTA or destination is unclear.

packs.name: Launch Coordination
packs.description: Coordinate writer, SEO, marketing, and operations around a launch so handoffs are clean and visible.
checklist: Define the launch objective, deliverables, owners, and due dates.
Sequence the work so briefs, drafts, reviews, promotion, and reporting do not collide.
Make each handoff explicit with entry criteria and exit criteria.
Track blockers, missed dependencies, and owner changes in one place.
Run a short post-launch review to capture what shipped, what slipped, and what to repeat.
handoffs: Pinchy: own the routing, sequencing, and blocker decisions.
Urchin: maintain owner/status tracking and red-yellow-green visibility.
Current, Coral, and Plankton: execute within the agreed handoff sequence.
escalation_rules: Escalate if key owners, deadlines, or deliverables are still ambiguous.
Escalate if one blocker stalls multiple specialists.

packs.name: Support Triage
packs.description: Handle first response, severity assessment, and escalation flow for customer issues.
checklist: Capture the issue, customer context, and current severity.
Send a fast first reply that sets expectation without overpromising.
Classify the issue into answer directly, queue, or immediate escalation.
Route engineering, billing, or operations follow-up to the correct owner.
Close the loop with the customer and log the pattern for future reuse.
handoffs: Anemone: own the customer-facing response and severity call.
Developer: take bug reports with reproduction steps.
Finance: handle billing or invoice correction work.
escalation_rules: Escalate immediately for P0/P1 issues or when customer impact is unclear.
Escalate if refunds, billing policy exceptions, or legal risk appear.

packs.name: Fact-Check Brief
packs.description: Turn a set of claims into a sourced internal brief with confidence markers and open questions.
checklist: Break the request into discrete claims or questions before searching.
Collect primary or strongest-available sources and note confidence by claim.
Separate confirmed facts, plausible inferences, and unknowns.
Compress the evidence into a short internal brief with citations.
Call out the highest-risk assumptions or missing data explicitly.
handoffs: Kelp: own research quality, sourcing, and confidence scoring.
Plankton: turn verified material into readable internal language if needed.
Coral: review for search-market implications when the topic is SEO-related.
escalation_rules: Escalate if no credible source base exists for a key claim.
Escalate if the brief mixes facts with business decisions that need approval.

packs.name: Code Review
packs.description: Run a reviewer-first pass for correctness, security, performance, and maintainability risks.
checklist: Confirm the intended behavior and compare it to the actual implementation.
Look for correctness, security, performance, and maintainability risks in that order.
Check whether tests cover new logic and important edge cases.
Turn findings into specific, actionable review comments.
Issue a clear approve or request-changes verdict.
handoffs: Barnacle: own the review and final verdict.
Chitin: address implementation and test gaps raised by review.
Reef: validate fixes before release if the patch is significant.
escalation_rules: Escalate immediately for security issues, data loss risks, or unclear requirements.
Escalate if the code change is too large to review confidently in one pass.

packs.name: Regression Gate
packs.description: Build a release-focused QA gate with regression scope, edge cases, and a clear pass/fail verdict.
checklist: Define the changed surface area and the highest-risk regression zones.
List happy-path, edge-case, and failure-mode checks explicitly.
Run or specify the tests needed before deployment.
Record any blockers, missing environments, or unclear specs.
Issue a PASS, FAIL, or BLOCKED verdict with reasons.
handoffs: Reef: own the test scope, execution, and verdict.
Barnacle: confirm review issues are addressed before QA closes.
Tide: only proceed with deployment after a clear PASS.
escalation_rules: Escalate if test environment gaps prevent a real verdict.
Escalate if the spec is unclear enough to invalidate the gate.

packs.name: Incident Response
packs.description: Coordinate rollback-first production incident handling with clear ownership and post-incident follow-up.
checklist: Assess severity fast and decide whether rollback should happen immediately.
Stabilize the system before root-cause analysis.
Assign owners for rollback, verification, communication, and follow-up.
Document the incident timeline, impact, and decision points.
Close with a post-incident review and concrete prevention actions.
handoffs: Tide: own rollback, deploy safety, and system stabilization.
Pinchy and Urchin: manage owner clarity, communication, and follow-up.
Reef: verify the post-fix state before reopening normal deployment flow.
escalation_rules: Escalate immediately for data loss, security impact, or unclear blast radius.
Escalate if rollback cannot be executed safely with current information.
